description:
## Background

The closed Workspace-management epic (`bsm-kia`) established backend-owned Workspace state, explicit command scope, atomic switching, catalog recovery, and desktop acceptance. A post-implementation audit found a durability defect and two follow-up hardening gaps: a failed persistence operation can leave mutable plugin-store state behind; the selector lacks direct save-failure recovery coverage; and production issue-command diagnostics bypass the app's native release logging policy.

## What to build

Harden Workspace persistence and diagnostics so a rejected Workspace transition can never become durable later, users have regression-proven save-failure recovery, and command diagnostics follow the native logging policy.

## Delivery graph

```mermaid
graph LR
  A[Make failed Workspace saves transactional] --> B[Prove Workspace save-failure recovery in the selector]
  C[Route Workspace command diagnostics through native logging]
```

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Failed Workspace save and reset operations cannot be persisted by a later save or application shutdown.
- [ ] A save failure preserves the prior visible Workspace snapshot and exposes the specified retryable selector feedback.
- [ ] Production Workspace command diagnostics do not bypass the release logging policy.

## Test Plan

- Each child adds focused regression coverage at its appropriate boundary.
- Run Rust, frontend, static-analysis, and relevant desktop acceptance checks after the child work lands.
title: Harden Workspace persistence and diagnostics
